agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement a loyalty points module in pure TypeScript (loyalty.ts) with two functions: earnPoints and checkExpiry. Bird has defined the domain rules and acceptance criteria; MJ has specified the architecture. One terminology conflict between Bird and MJ has been resolved below regarding the expiry window calculation.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"A customer earns 1 point per dollar spent, rounded down to the nearest integer (floor rounding).","testable_assertion":"earnPoints(customerId, 47.80) returns 47; earnPoints(customerId, 0.99) returns 0; earnPoints(customerId, 100.00) returns 100"},{"rule":"Points expire after 12 months of account inactivity, defined as no purchase in the last 12 calendar months.","testable_assertion":"An account whose last purchase was exactly 12 calendar months ago or longer has all points marked expired. An account whose last purchase was 11 months and 29 days ago retains its points."}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Pure TypeScript module with no external dependencies","implementation_note":"Create a single loyalty.ts file exporting earnPoints(customerId: string, purchaseAmount: number) and checkExpiry(customerId: string). No frameworks, no database calls — pure functions."},{"decision":"Use Math.floor for point calculation","implementation_note":"earnPoints should return Math.floor(purchaseAmount). Negative or zero amounts should return 0 (defensive guard)."},{"decision":"Use calendar-month comparison for expiry, NOT 365 days","implementation_note":"checkExpiry must compare last_purchase_date against the current date minus 12 calendar months (e.g., use Date and setMonth(getMonth() - 12) or equivalent). Do NOT subtract 365 days — see contradictions_resolved below for rationale."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"AC1: Floor rounding on normal purchase","given":"A purchase of $47.80","when":"earnPoints(customerId, 47.80) is called","then":"47 points are earned"},{"criterion":"AC2: Sub-dollar purchase yields zero points","given":"A purchase of $0.99","when":"earnPoints(customerId, 0.99) is called","then":"0 points are earned"},{"criterion":"AC3: Points expire after 12 months inactivity","given":"An account with no purchase in the last 12 calendar months","when":"checkExpiry(customerId) is called","then":"All points for that customer are marked expired"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"account inactivity","technical_term":"last_purchase_date comparison","definition":"Inactivity means the customer's most recent purchase date is 12 or more calendar months before the current date. This is the sole trigger for expiry — no other activity (logins, browsing) resets the clock."},{"domain_term":"points marked expired","technical_term":"expiry status flag or return value","definition":"checkExpiry should return or set a clear indication that all accumulated points are expired. Shaq to decide representation (boolean return, status enum, or mutation) — but the domain intent is binary: all points expire at once, not partially."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird specifies '12 months of account inactivity' while MJ specifies 'current date minus 365 days'. These are not equivalent — 12 calendar months can be 365, 366, or vary due to month-length differences and leap years.","resolution":"Bird is the domain authority. The rule is 12 calendar months, not 365 days. Implementation must use calendar-month arithmetic (e.g., subtracting 12 from the month component) rather than subtracting a fixed number of days. This preserves correct behavior across leap years and variable-length months."}],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Point earning logic is unambiguous and fully specified by Bird","Architecture is simple with no external dependencies","The 12-months vs 365-days conflict has a clear resolution: Bird's domain rule takes precedence"],"low_confidence_areas":["checkExpiry return type and side effects are unspecified — Shaq has latitude here","Edge case: what happens if purchaseAmount is negative (not addressed by Bird)","How last_purchase_date is stored/retrieved is out of scope but Shaq will need a data access pattern or injected dependency"],"assumptions":["earnPoints is a pure calculation that returns a number; persistence of points is handled elsewhere","checkExpiry needs access to last_purchase_date — assumed to be injected or looked up, not hardcoded","Negative purchase amounts should defensively return 0 points (not specified by Bird but safe default)"]}}
